pages.text: I can heal quickly on a full stomach, so alimentation is key, but I can't always rely on food. I should always keep supplies for quick boosts and sustained healing.$(br2)$(#a84b0f)$(italics)Your saturation (yellow outline on hunger bar shanks) heals you, but it slows down when near depleted, and being full prevents recharging it.$()
In the following pages, I list a number of remedies, drugs, and elixirs that I have researched or heard about.

pages.title: Not milk and veggies
pages.text: This mix of $(t:sourced from Stomachs, dropped by cows)$(thing)enzymes$() and moss, both of which have healing qualities, tastes worse than ferrought but is an effective source of regeneration, if I can stomach it. I might not be able to defend myself during the $(t:short blindness, vulnerability, and nausea)$(thing)aftertaste$().

pages.title: Slow Healing
pages.text: Clean bandages of durable fiber sterilized in water, $(bold)reliable$(). They provide an $(t:6 HP)$(thing)instant boost$(). Preparing them requires some initial setup with a kettle, and applying them during combat would be foolish. Something to hold on to, until I'm out of danger.

Bandages with a healing salve distilled from $(t:found commonly in caves under forests and other places)$(item)Moss$(), $(t:a plant native to swampy areas)$(item)Trillium$(), $(t:the seed for eucalyptus saplings)$(item)Eucalyptus Capsule$(), $(t:common to dry or sandy environments, but found almost everywhere)$(item)Aloe Vera$(), $(t:Extremely rarely found deep underground)$(item)Blue Glowshroom$(), and $(t:commonly found in most caves)$(item)Cave Root$(), heals more and continues healing.
The best first aid kit that combines practical medicine with non-standard healing techniques. Big drawbacks but lots of health.

pages.item: rustic:elixir{display: {LocName: 'Metabolic Whey'}, ElixirEffects: [{Effect: 'minecraft:regeneration', Duration: 160, Amplifier: 2}, {Effect: 'minecraft:hunger', Duration: 160, Amplifier: 29}, {Effect: 'minecraft:nausea', Duration: 160, Amplifier: 0}]}
pages.title: First aid
pages.text: This is a rather cheap but relatively dangerous concoction, yet more bearable than Tripe Musguette. I just need to have some food before and after it; large healing boost for large amounts of calories.

pages.title: Panacea
pages.text: A powerful and expensive elixir that quickly restores most people to a $(t:10 HP)$(thing)healthy state$().$(br2)The value of healing elixirs lies in their speed, and lack of drawbacks; but the impracticality to create them en masse means they are more of a commodity.

Ambrosia is the mythical cure-all of divine origin, cause of great joy and discord. As far as I am aware, this rock-like object was real deal but is also equally noxious. I include it here as it may cure $(t:Emancipated Ambrosia has a 80% chance to cure the plague and guarantees recovering any lost hearts from dying to the plague)$(thing)other afflictions$().
